docs.title: apifetch
docs.text:
A small, dependency-light toolkit for talking to token-authenticated REST APIs. It handles three recurring chores:

  1. Token management — store/get/remove/list tokens in process environment variables (never written to disk), namespaced per service.
  2. Request building — pluggable authentication and pagination strategies, bundled into a reusable Api profile.
  3. Data retrieval — fetch one page, or fetch everything in chunks.

This is the Python sibling of the R package apifetch.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
import apifetch as af

api = af.Api(
    endpoint="https://api.example.com/v1/search",
    service="Example",
    auth=af.AuthBearer(),                 # "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
    pagination=af.PaginateOffset(where="query"),
)

af.store_token("reports", "my-secret-token", service="Example")

one_page = af.fetch(api, "reports", limit=50)
everything = af.fetch_all(api, "reports", chunk_size=1000)

docs.title: Strategies
docs.text:
Authentication: AuthBearer, AuthRaw, AuthHeader, AuthQuery.

Pagination: PaginateOffset(where="header" | "query"), PaginateNone.

See the BigDataPE use case for a real-world configuration, or the full API reference.

docs.title: Use case: the Big Data PE API
docs.text: apifetch was extracted from the BigDataPE package, which wraps the Big Data PE platform — a public-data REST API run by the Government of the State of Pernambuco, Brazil. This page is the canonical worked example of configuring the package for a real API.

docs.title: What is specific about Big Data PE
docs.text: Convention Strategy Token sent verbatim in the Authorization header (no Bearer) AuthRaw() limit/offset sent as HTTP headers, not query params PaginateOffset(where="header") Responses carry a status column "Mensagem" to drop drop_cols=("Mensagem",) Reachable only from the PE Conectado network / VPN connect_hint=...

docs.title: Defining the profile
docs.text:
import apifetch as af

bigdatape = af.Api(
    endpoint="https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar",
    service="BigDataPE",
    auth=af.AuthRaw(),
    pagination=af.PaginateOffset(where="header"),
    drop_cols=("Mensagem",),
    connect_hint="Ensure you are on the PE Conectado network or VPN.",
)

docs.title: Storing a token and fetching
docs.text:
af.store_token("dengue", "your-token-here", service="BigDataPE")

# A single page of 50 records
dengue = af.fetch(bigdatape, "dengue", limit=50)

# Everything, in chunks, with a progress message per chunk
dengue_all = af.fetch_all(bigdatape, "dengue", chunk_size=50_000, verbosity=1)

On language

Function and parameter names are English, but the API's response keys and some values are Portuguese (e.g. nu_notificacao, "BOA VIAGEM"). That is intentional — they come straight from the upstream service.

docs.title: Fetching
docs.text:
Data fetching: single page and chunked retrieval.

Fetch every record by paging through api in chunks.

Iteratively calls :func:fetch with an advancing offset until a chunk comes back empty or total_limit is reached. Columns listed in api.drop_cols are removed from each record.

docs.title: ApiError
docs.text:
Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when the API is unreachable or returns an HTTP error.

docs.title: fetch(api, name, limit=None, offset=0, query=None, verbosity=0, client=None)
docs.text:
Fetch a single page from api and return it as a list of records.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default api Api

An :class:Api profile.

required name str

Token name to authenticate with (looked up via api.service).

required limit Optional[float]

Maximum records to request. None/inf means no limit.

None offset int

Starting record (omitted when 0).

0 query Optional[dict]

Extra query-string filters.

None verbosity int

0 silent, >=1 progress messages.

0 client Optional[Client]

Optional pre-built httpx.Client (useful for testing or connection reuse). One is created and closed per call otherwise.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

The parsed JSON body as a list of dictionaries.

docs.title: fetch_all(api, name, total_limit=None, chunk_size=50000, query=None, verbosity=0, client=None)
docs.text:
Fetch every record by paging through api in chunks.

Iteratively calls :func:fetch with an advancing offset until a chunk comes back empty or total_limit is reached. Columns listed in api.drop_cols are removed from each record.

docs.title: API profile
docs.text:
Describe an API endpoint together with its auth and pagination strategies.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default endpoint str

The base API URL.

required service str

Namespace used to look up the token (see :func:get_token).

'apifetch' auth Auth

An :class:Auth strategy. Defaults to bearer-token auth.

AuthBearer() pagination Pagination

A :class:Pagination strategy. Defaults to offset paging sent as HTTP headers.

PaginateOffset() drop_cols tuple[str, ...]

Keys to drop from each record after parsing (e.g. a status column).

() connect_hint str | None

Optional extra line shown on a connection error (e.g. a VPN requirement).

None

docs.title: Authentication strategies
docs.text:
Bases: Auth

Send the token verbatim in a header (default Authorization).

This is what the Big Data PE API expects.

Bases: Auth

Send "<prefix><token>" in a header (default Authorization: Bearer).

Bases: Auth

Send the token in an arbitrary header (e.g. X-API-Key).

Bases: Auth

Send the token as a URL query parameter.

docs.title: Pagination strategies
docs.text:
Bases: Pagination

Send limit/offset as HTTP headers (default) or query parameters.

Non-positive, None and infinite values are omitted.

Bases: Pagination

Send no pagination parameters.

docs.title: Token management
docs.text:
Store token for name in an environment variable.

Refuses to overwrite an existing, non-empty variable.

Return the token stored for name/service, or None if missing.

Remove the token stored for name/service if present.

Return the names (without the service prefix) of stored tokens.

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when the API is unreachable or returns an HTTP error.
